checks.browser_safe_core: pass (dist/index.js + dist/element.js: no static node/mermaid/jsdom/resvg imports)
checks.hostile_label_probe: pass (0 breakouts across svg + html for all 3 types)

summary: Round-2 review of the three NEW native re-skinned renderers (sequence/class/state). Verdict APPROVE — no open blockers or majors. Security is clean: a full hostile-label probe (script/quote/CDATA/onerror/generic payloads in participant, message, class-name, member/method, relation-label, state-name and transition-label positions) shows every user-derived string escaped at every SVG text/attribute sink, the interactive DOM path using textContent, and the HTML JSON payload escaping '<'; the v1 style-sanitization + zero-network HTML regressions still pass. Determinism verified (byte-identical re-renders), browser-safe core intact in the built dist (all mermaid/jsdom/resvg imports lazy), vnmRuntime reuse sound (dom-runtime-parity green), and the FR4/FR5 md-unavailable contract for class/state is correct (exit 0, non-zero only --strict). Three new minor/nit fidelity findings, all AGENT-FIXABLE: REV-005 (state names ending in start/end misclassified as pseudo-states, labels dropped), REV-006 (class relation endpoints silently mis-paired on ambiguous underscore-name splits), REV-007 (unlabeled sequence messages keep mermaid's U+200B placeholder, drawing a stray label plate). The four round-1 findings (REV-001..004) are re-verified closed. Route: advance to test; batch the three minors into the hands-on pass.
